steps.title: Use the x-intercept definition
steps.content: An $x$-intercept is a point where the graph touches or crosses the $x$-axis. At that point, the $y$-coordinate is $0$.

steps.title: Read the graph
steps.content: The parabola touches the $x$-axis at one point. The contact point is on the positive side of the axis, at the tick mark labeled $4$.

steps.title: Match the point
steps.content: The point with $x=4$ and $y=0$ is $(4,0)$. The answer is D.
